steps.title: Separate the clauses
steps.content: The first clause says the Limón technique is known for weight and weightlessness. The next clause begins dancers may explore. Two independent clauses need stronger punctuation than a comma or nothing.

steps.title: Use the semicolon
steps.content: B creates an awkward relative-clause structure, C makes a comma splice, and D fuses the clauses with no punctuation. A semicolon correctly links the two related independent clauses. The answer is A.
